overview: A four-color artifact synergy deck that leverages Breya’s mana generation and +1/+1 pumping abilities to fuel token swarms, combat tricks, and artifact synergies. It aims to stabilize early, out-value opponents through card advantage and board presence, and close games via combat damage or triggered abilities.
win_conditions: Pumping a high-impact artifact (Myr Battlesphere, Thopter Assembly, or a token generator) for lethal.
Using Skullclamp + Breya to draw cards and stabilize.
Triggering win conditions via artifact ETB or combat damage.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one mana rock (Sol Ring, Arcane Signet, Mind Stone), and Breya or a key synergist (Myr Battlesphere, Thopter Assembly, Skullclamp).
Prioritize hands with protection or value engines.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan on 4+ lands with no ramp, or 3+ rocks with no lands.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, drop a mana rock if possible.
Turn 2: Cast Breya or a synergist.
Sequence lands to ensure Breya drops by turn 2 or 3.
Tap Breya for mana and pump a key artifact to trigger ETB effects, stabilize, or set up a lethal threat.
If you have a token doublers or artifact synergist, prioritize setting those up before overcommitting to combat.
mid_game: Hoard mana for Breya’s pumping abilities and key synergies.
Avoid overcommitting to pumps without a clear plan or protection.
Keep interaction in hand for sweepers or targeted hate.
Graveyard is secondary but watch for recursion or sacrifice outlets.
Commit threats when you have protection (hexproof, indestructible, counters) or removal ready.
Hold back if facing sweepers or heavy artifact hate.
Use Breya to pump blockers for favorable trades or to block key threats.
late_game: Pumping a high-impact artifact (Myr Battlesphere, Thopter Assembly, or a token generator) for lethal.
Using Skullclamp + Breya to draw cards and stabilize.
Triggering win conditions via artifact ETB or combat damage.
Go all-in when you have protection, the opponent lacks interaction, or pumping will trigger a win condition.
Commit when you can trade up or force a favorable combat math.

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: over-pumping without a plan, ignoring opponent’s interaction, or failing to protect Breya.
Unique interaction: Breya’s abilities can target your own artifacts in response to triggers.
Watch for artifact hate like Disenchant or Abrade.
Protect Breya at all costs, as it’s the deck’s primary engine and win condition enabler.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, efficient threats. Pump artifacts in response to counters. Use cantrips to dig through counters. Favor artifact-based win cons that don’t rely on casting spells.
Apply early pressure. Use removal on their mana dorks. Don’t let them stabilize. Use sweepers if they get too big.
Stabilize with removal and board wipes. Use Breya to pump blockers. Gain life if possible. Don’t overextend into combat.
Pressure with efficient artifacts. Use Breya to pump and trade. Dig for combo pieces or extra turns. Force them to use interaction on low-value threats.
Value engines and card draw win. Prioritize removing their Breya or key synergists. Use targeted removal over sweepers. Control the board with efficient pumps.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath, Toxic Deluge), artifact hate (Disenchant, Abrade), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells.
Track if they’re specifically targeting Breya or your key synergists.
